commit_summaries.message:
feat(spec): add Appendix A — Grimoire

Twelve patterns (A.1–A.12) showing what you build with the spec's
vocabulary, from a raw LLM query through the familiar. Closes with
A.13 on what makes a teaching example vs conformance theater.

Synthesized from six drafts across three models (Claude, Codex, Gemini)
over two prompt iterations. Informative, not normative — no new
conformance rules beyond chapters 1–7.

feat(py): grimoire teaching examples

Replace 16 conformance-theater examples with 12 grimoire-aligned
teaching examples (A.1–A.12). Each example visibly constructs its
components, uses realistic intents, inspects output metadata, and
shows non-happy paths. FakeLLM is a CI fallback, not the only mode.

feat(clj): grimoire teaching examples

Replace old examples with 13 grimoire-aligned teaching functions
(A.1–A.12 + ACP). Visible construction, spec rule references
(CIRCLE-1, CIRCLE-2), dual-mode LLM, loom inspection, error paths.

feat(ex): grimoire teaching examples

Replace 16 conformance-theater examples with 12 grimoire-aligned
teaching examples. Visible construction with pattern matching,
error steering, loom inspection, persistent entity, familiar pattern.
CLI catalog integration preserved.

fix(py): use public API in gate/medium examples, fix meta intent

02_gate: replace private _execute_gate() with cast_with_thread()
06_medium: replace cantrip._make_tools() with medium_for().make_tools()
10_loom: natural intent instead of "do not finish"

fix(clj): add LLM override to composition/ACP, rule IDs in docstrings

09-composition and 13-acp now accept llm-config override instead of
being FakeLLM-only. All 13 docstrings now reference their primary
spec rule IDs (LLM-1, CIRCLE-1, WARD-1, etc.).

fix(ex): rewrite examples with inline construction, rule IDs

Delete all opaque helpers (cantrip_from, identity_for, build_basic).
Every example now constructs Cantrip.new() inline with visible
llm/identity/circle fields. Spec rule IDs in comments throughout.
Realistic intents, meaningful persistent entity, children use LLM
resolution instead of hardcoded FakeLLM.

fix(py): make examples work with real LLMs, add InProcessPythonExecutor

Framework changes:
- Add InProcessPythonExecutor: runs entity Python via exec() with gate
  functions injected, replacing MiniCodeExecutor (regex toy) as default
- Give done gate a default parameter schema so LLMs know answer is required
- Set tool_choice="required" when require_done_tool=True
- Children get generic system prompt instead of inheriting parent's
  delegation instructions (which reference gates unavailable at depth 0)
- SQLiteLoomStore: check_same_thread=False for threaded executor
- Add Exception types to safe builtins for try/except in entity code
- Support timeout_s in medium_depends for InProcessPythonExecutor

Example changes (all three languages):
- Kill silent LLM fallbacks: missing env vars raise, not silently use FakeLLM
- Identity prompts name gates explicitly, no template-copying bait
- Scripted code-medium responses use tool_calls format (gate="code")
- All 12 Python examples verified working with real LLM

Tests: 227 py, 86 clj, 168 ex — all passing

fix(clj,ex): framework fixes for real LLM compatibility

Clojure:
- done gate gets answer parameter schema (gates.clj)
- done-observation accepts string keys from real LLM JSON (circle.clj)
- tool-call-id propagated through observations (circle.clj)
- tool-call-id included in tool response messages (runtime.clj)
- tool_choice auto-set to :required for require-done-tool (runtime.clj)
- code medium turns synthesize tool-calls for OpenAI message format (runtime.clj)
- schemas always include "properties" field (llm.clj)
- FakeLLM skips tool_choice enforcement (llm.clj)
- dotenv loading for .env files in examples (examples.clj)
- structural tests rewritten with 261 assertions (examples_test.clj)

Elixir:
- done gate gets answer parameter schema (circle.ex)
- framework-level tests for done schema and child identity (examples_test.exs)

Verified with real LLM (gpt-5-mini): Clojure examples 01, 04, 07, 09, 12.
Elixir examples 01, 04. All scripted tests pass (110 clj, 170 ex).

feat(py,clj): add capability text for code medium prompt synthesis

Code medium now injects sandbox physics and host function documentation
into the LLM context, matching TS's capabilityDocs() pattern.

Python: capability_text() on CodeMedium generates sandbox docs + gate
descriptions; runtime injects as first system message for code medium.

Clojure: capability-text, tool-view, extract-code for single 'clojure'
tool pattern; SCI sandbox now includes exception classes; capability text
correctly describes fire-and-forget gate model.

Verified with gpt-5-mini: py examples 07/09 and clj examples 07/09
complete in 1-3 turns with correct function names and behavior.
chore: some nits

fix(py): update spec coverage test regex for new SPEC format

SPEC.md switched from blockquote rule format (> **RULE-1**:) to
bare line format (RULE-1:). Update the regex to match.

feat(spec): v0.3.0 — tighten spec, reformat rules

Spec revision:
- 17,062 → 11,528 words (−32%), all 77 behavioral rules preserved
- Consolidated repeated definitions to single canonical locations
- Cut self-narration and redundant prose that paraphrased adjacent rules
- Compressed entity emergence passage, folding section, gate presentation
- Converted Grimoire patterns to recipe cards (What to notice / Substitution)
- Preserved voice: entity emergence, lethal trifecta, ghost library, Gargoyles epigraph
- Sacred passages kept at full strength, redundant restatements cut
- PROD-4 folding threshold softened from SHOULD to MAY

Formatting:
- All 77 behavioral rules reformatted from blockquotes to fenced code blocks
- Version bumped 0.2.0 → 0.3.0

fix(py): subtractive builtins ward, expose call_gate, allow system_prompt

- Replace _SAFE_BUILTINS allowlist with subtractive _BUILTIN_WARDS set
  (start with full builtins, subtract dangerous ones — matches spec's
  ward model)
- Expose call_gate() in executor namespace to match capability_text
- Add system_prompt to call_entity validator allowlist
- Document timeout as best-effort (CPython thread limitation)

feat(py): support context key in call_entity delegation

When a parent entity passes context to call_entity, prepend it to the
child's intent string so the child sees the data. Matches TS behavior
where parent_context is forwarded to children.

feat(py): rewrite all 12 grimoire examples as teaching programs

Every example now:
- Has narrative print() output that tells the story as it runs
- Uses realistic financial/SaaS intents instead of toy arithmetic
- Shares LLM resolution via _llm.py (scripted for CI, real for demos)
- References spec rule IDs in comments (CIRCLE-1, WARD-1, etc.)
- Still returns dict for test compatibility (227 pass, 2 skip)

Verified with real LLM: patterns 01 and 12 (familiar) confirmed working.
The familiar successfully delegates to 5+ specialist children via code
medium, each doing real Q3 SaaS metrics analysis.

feat(clj,ex): support context and system_prompt in call_entity

Both Clojure and Elixir now accept :context and :system-prompt keys
in call_entity/call-agent requests, matching Python behavior:
- context: prepended to child intent as "Context: ...\n\nTask: ..."
- system_prompt: overrides child identity instead of inheriting parent's

feat(clj,ex): rewrite grimoire examples as teaching programs

All examples in both Clojure (13) and Elixir (12) now have:
- Realistic SaaS/financial intents instead of toy math
- Rich narrative print output explaining each step
- Spec rule IDs in comments
- Realistic scripted responses for CI mode

Test baselines preserved:
- Clojure: 110 tests, 261 assertions, 0 failures
- Elixir: 170 tests, 0 failures

fix(clj,ex): bump LLM timeout defaults to 120s

30s (ex) and 60s (clj) were too short for code medium with real LLMs.
Discovered during real LLM verification of rewritten examples — Elixir
example 07 (error steering) consistently timed out at 30s.

- Clojure: llm.clj default 60000 -> 120000
- Elixir: cantrip.ex llm_from_env default 30_000 -> 120_000
- Elixir: openai_compatible.ex default 30_000 -> 120_000
- Elixir: entity_server.ex batch child timeout 30_000 -> 120_000

fix(clj,ex,py): framework fixes for child entity delegation and code medium

Clojure:
- Replace hand-rolled JSON parser with clojure.data.json (fixes potential
  escape handling bugs in LLM response parsing)
- Set max_completion_tokens: 16384 default (prevents output truncation)
- Improve SCI capability text: document Java interop prohibition,
  synchronous call-agent semantics, SCI-specific limitations
- Give children generic system prompt instead of inheriting parent's
  coordinator identity (matches TS reference implementation)
- Strip delegation gates (call_entity/call_entity_batch) from children
- Cap child max-turns at 3 to bound error cascade duration
- Rewrite example 12 prompts with :system-prompt in call-agent calls

Elixir:
- Fix put_in on Cantrip struct (use struct update syntax instead)
- Give children generic system prompt (same fix as Clojure)
- Add defmodule prohibition to capability_presentation
- Rewrite example 12 to use call_entity.() binding
- Relax example 12 test assertion for loom turn count

Python:
- Fix example 07 to use code medium (spec A.7 requirement)
- Replace trivial intents in example 06 with realistic SaaS domain
- Tighten example 12 familiar system prompt
- Add docstring to example 08
fix: address review comments

Add normalize_message, normalize_tool_spec, normalize_tool_call helpers
and apply them at the entry point of each LLM adapter. Removes all
defensive dual-access patterns (atom || string key) from Anthropic,
Gemini, and OpenAI Compatible adapters.
